aspects.description: Health check endpoint returns correct response
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET /health (no /api prefix, no authentication) returns 200 OK with a JSON body whose status indicates ok, plus timestamp and version fields, e.g. {"status": "ok", "timestamp": "2026-03-25T15:28:34.755Z", "version": "1.0.0"}.

aspects.description: Content service connects to and uses the provided database
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET /api/courses returns course data from the provided database dump (not empty, not hardcoded). Confirm at least one known seed course slug is present (e.g. tailwind-css-shadcn-ui-tutorial or web-development-fundamentals).

aspects.description: Database schema is not modified
aspects.extraDescription: Verify: Restore the original content-service database dump and confirm the service still works correctly. Alternatively, inspect the schema for any added, removed, or altered tables or columns against the ER diagram in project-description.md.

aspects.description: All error responses return JSON with at least an error and code field
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Trigger any error (e.g. GET /api/courses/nonexistent-slug with a valid token). Verify the response body is application/json and contains both an error (human-readable message) and a code (machine-readable string, e.g. COURSE_NOT_FOUND) field.

aspects.description: 401 Unauthorized returned when Bearer token is missing
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Send GET /api/courses/{valid-slug} with no Authorization header. Verify the response is 401 with {"error": "Unauthorized", "code": "UNAUTHORIZED"}.

aspects.description: 401 Unauthorized returned for invalid or malformed token
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Send GET /api/courses/{valid-slug} with Authorization: Bearer invalidtoken123. Verify 401 with code UNAUTHORIZED. Also send a structurally valid token signed with a wrong secret and verify 401.

aspects.description: 401 Unauthorized returned for an expired token
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Craft a token with a valid signature but an exp value in the past (e.g. exp: 1). Send it to a protected endpoint. Verify 401 with code UNAUTHORIZED.

aspects.description: 403 Forbidden returned when user is not enrolled in the requested course
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Send GET /api/courses/{valid-slug} with a valid token for a user who is not enrolled in that course. Verify 403 with {"error": "Not enrolled in this course", "code": "NOT_ENROLLED"}.

aspects.description: 403 Forbidden returned when a chapter is locked (previous chapter not completed)
aspects.extraDescription: Test: With an enrolled user, request a chapter with orderIndex > 1 without having completed the previous chapter. Verify 403 with {"error": "Previous chapter must be completed first", "code": "CHAPTER_LOCKED"}.

aspects.description: 404 Not Found returned for a non-existent course
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET /api/courses/this-course-does-not-exist with a valid token. Verify 404 with {"error": "Course not found", "code": "COURSE_NOT_FOUND"}.

aspects.description: 404 Not Found returned for a non-existent chapter
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET /api/courses/{valid-slug}/chapters/99999 with a valid enrolled-user token. Verify 404 with {"error": "Chapter not found", "code": "CHAPTER_NOT_FOUND"}.

aspects.description: Token structure is validated (must have exactly three parts: header.payload.signature)
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Send requests with malformed tokens: one-part (justpayload), two-part (header.payload), and four-part (a.b.c.d). All must return 401 UNAUTHORIZED.

aspects.description: HMAC-SHA256 signature is verified using the correct shared secret
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Create a valid token structure (correct exp, sub) but sign it with a different secret and verify 401. Then sign the same header.payload with the correct shared secret (38344ac35d91bfd0c8f43963b0ca188d2a039504e825ff968b0366855bdbca5b) and verify the protected request succeeds (200 for an enrolled user).

aspects.description: Token expiry (exp claim) is validated on every request
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Issue a token with exp set to now-1 (past) and verify 401. Issue a token with exp set to now+3600 and verify a protected endpoint returns 200 (for an enrolled user).

aspects.description: User ID is extracted from the sub claim of the token payload
aspects.extraDescription: Verify: The content service uses the sub value from the token payload as the user identifier when calling main backend endpoints (e.g. enrolled-courses, completed-chapters). Issue tokens for different sub values and confirm enrollment/completion checks resolve to the correct user (e.g. a token for an enrolled user succeeds where a token for a non-enrolled user gets 403 NOT_ENROLLED on the same course).

aspects.description: Token validation is performed locally without external JWT or auth libraries
aspects.extraDescription: Review: Inspect the submitted code and its dependency manifest (package.json / composer.json / requirements) for JWT or auth libraries (e.g. jsonwebtoken, passport-jwt, firebase/php-jwt, python-jose). Validation must be custom-implemented using a standard HMAC-SHA256 primitive only; no external JWT/auth library may be used.

aspects.description: Token validated locally on every protected request (no caching or session state)
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Send an expired token to two different protected endpoints in succession; both must return 401. Send a valid token to two different protected endpoints; both must succeed. Confirm no request is accepted based on prior session state rather than the token itself.

aspects.description: GET /api/courses returns all courses without authentication
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET /api/courses with no Authorization header. Verify 200 OK with a non-empty courses array populated from the provided database (not hardcoded).

aspects.description: Course objects in list contain all required fields
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET /api/courses. For each course in the courses array, verify the presence of id (integer), slug (string), title (string), description (string), difficulty (beginner|intermediate|advanced), totalChapters (integer), totalCredits (integer), createdAt.

aspects.description: Each course in the list includes a chapters array ordered by orderIndex
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET /api/courses. For a course with multiple chapters, verify the chapters array is present and items appear in ascending orderIndex order. Each chapter item must include id, title, credits, orderIndex.

aspects.description: GET /api/courses/:slug returns single course details (Bearer token required)
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET /api/courses/{valid-slug} with a valid token for an enrolled user. Verify 200 with a course object wrapped in a course key, including id, slug, title, difficulty, totalChapters, totalCredits, and a chapters array.

aspects.description: isCompleted flag for each chapter is fetched from the main backend
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET /api/courses/{slug} for a user who has completed some chapters. Verify each chapter includes isCompleted: true for completed chapters and false for incomplete ones, matching the main backend /users/me/completed-chapters response. Complete a chapter via the main backend and confirm the flag flips on the next request.

aspects.description: User enrollment is validated with the main backend before serving course details
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET /api/courses/{slug} with a valid token for a user NOT enrolled in that course. Verify 403 NOT_ENROLLED. Then enroll the user (via the dashboard/main backend) and confirm the same request returns 200, proving the check uses the main backend /users/me/enrolled-courses data rather than local state.

aspects.description: GET /api/courses/:slug/chapters/:chapterId returns chapter metadata
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET /api/courses/{slug}/chapters/{id} for an accessible chapter (e.g. the first chapter of the tailwind-css-shadcn-ui-tutorial course). Verify the top-level response (not wrapped) includes courseId, chapterId, title, description, and credits.

aspects.description: Content blocks are returned in orderIndex order
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET a chapter with multiple content blocks. Verify the content array items are ordered by ascending orderIndex and that each item exposes an orderIndex field matching its position.

aspects.description: Heading blocks (h1-h4) are returned with correct type and text fields
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET a chapter containing h1, h2, h3, or h4 blocks. Verify each heading item has type ("h1"/"h2"/"h3"/"h4") and text (the heading content from the text column), with no unrelated block fields.

aspects.description: Paragraph and list_item blocks are assembled from chunks into HTML
aspects.extraDescription:
Test: GET a chapter with paragraph and list_item blocks that have associated chunks. Verify: (1) the html field is present with rendered HTML; (2) bold chunks are wrapped in ; (3) italic chunks are wrapped in ; (4) paragraph blocks produce

...

; (5) list_item blocks produce
  • ...
    • . Chunk rows must NOT appear directly in the response.

aspects.description: rawText field is included for paragraph and list_item blocks
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET a chapter with paragraph/list_item blocks. Verify each such block has a rawText field (may be null when the raw_text column is null, but must not be omitted entirely).

aspects.description: Image blocks are returned with url and alt fields
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET a chapter containing an image block. Verify the item has type "image", a url (from the url column), and an alt field (from the img_alt column), with no fields from other block types.

aspects.description: Video blocks are returned with url and title fields
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET a chapter containing a video block. Verify the item has type "video", a url, and a title field, with no raw text or heading fields.

aspects.description: Link blocks are returned with url and title fields
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET a chapter containing a link block. Verify the item has type "link", a url, and a title field.

aspects.description: Quiz is included in the chapter response with questions and options
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET a chapter with a quiz. Verify: (1) the response contains a quiz.questions array; (2) each question has id, text, and an options array; (3) each option has id and text.

aspects.description: credits field is included in the chapter response
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET a chapter. Verify the top-level response includes a credits field (integer) matching the chapter's credit value in the database.

aspects.description: Correct answers (isCorrect) are never exposed to clients in any response
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET /api/courses/{slug}/chapters/{id}. Inspect every option object in quiz.questions. Verify no option contains isCorrect, correct, answer, or any similar field. Exposing correct answers is a strict security failure for this aspect.

aspects.description: First chapter (orderIndex 1) is always accessible for enrolled users
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET the first chapter (orderIndex 1) of a course with a valid enrolled-user token and no completed chapters. Verify 200 OK (not 403 CHAPTER_LOCKED).

aspects.description: Chapters with orderIndex > 1 are locked if the previous chapter is not completed
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET a chapter with orderIndex 2 or higher for an enrolled user who has not completed the preceding chapter. Verify 403 CHAPTER_LOCKED. Then mark the previous chapter complete (via the main backend) and retry; verify 200.

aspects.description: Previous chapter completion is fetched from the main backend
aspects.extraDescription: Verify: Sequential access uses the main backend /users/me/completed-chapters endpoint to decide whether the previous chapter is completed; the content service must not store completion state itself. Complete chapter 1 via the main backend and confirm chapter 2 becomes accessible in the content service.

aspects.description: POST .../quiz/validate accepts and processes an answers array
aspects.extraDescription: Test: POST /api/courses/{slug}/chapters/{id}/quiz/validate with body {"answers": [{"questionId": 1, "selectedOptionId": "a"}, ...]}. Verify the endpoint accepts the request and returns a 200 result object (not 400/404).

aspects.description: Returns {"passed": true} when all submitted answers are correct
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Submit all correct answers for a chapter quiz. Verify 200 OK with body {"passed": true}.

aspects.description: Returns {"passed": false} when one or more answers are incorrect
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Submit at least one wrong answer for a chapter quiz. Verify 200 OK with body {"passed": false}.

aspects.description: Correct answers validated against quiz_options.isCorrect for every question
aspects.extraDescription: Verify: Answers are cross-referenced with the is_correct column in quiz_options. Passing requires every submitted answer to match its correct option; submitting all-but-one correct must return passed: false (not passed on majority or first match).

aspects.description: On a passing quiz, the content service notifies the main backend chapter completion endpoint
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Submit all correct answers for a chapter. After the call, verify the chapter appears as completed on the main backend (check /users/me/completed-chapters). The content service must call POST /courses/:id/chapters/:id/complete on the main backend when passed is true.

aspects.description: Main backend completion failure after a pass is handled gracefully
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Force the main backend completion call to fail (e.g. stop/misconfigure the main backend) and submit a passing quiz. Verify the service responds with 502 or 503, still reports {"passed": true}, and includes code CHAPTER_COMPLETION_FAILED rather than a generic 500 or a silent success.

aspects.description: POST /api/courses creates a new course with required fields (title, slug)
aspects.extraDescription: Test: POST /api/courses with {"title": "Test Course", "slug": "test-course"} and a valid Bearer token. Verify the course is created and persisted (retrievable via GET /api/courses).

aspects.description: POST /api/courses returns 201 Created with the course object
aspects.extraDescription: Test: POST /api/courses with valid data. Verify status 201 and a response body containing a course object with id, slug, title, description, difficulty, totalChapters, totalCredits, and chapters.

aspects.description: Slug format is validated (lowercase letters, digits, hyphens only)
aspects.extraDescription: Test: POST /api/courses with slugs containing invalid characters: "My Course" (space), "UPPER", "special@char". Each must return 400 with code INVALID_COURSE_SLUG. Confirm a valid slug like "my-new-course-2" returns 201.

aspects.description: Duplicate slug is rejected with 409 Conflict
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Create a course with slug "test-course", then POST again with the same slug. Verify 409 with code DUPLICATE_COURSE_SLUG.

aspects.description: Optional fields (description, difficulty) are handled in POST /api/courses
aspects.extraDescription: Test: POST with difficulty: "intermediate" and description: "A test desc"; verify both appear in the response. Test an invalid difficulty value returns 400. Test that omitting description sets it to null (default difficulty is beginner).

aspects.description: New course is initialized with totalChapters: 0, totalCredits: 0, chapters: []
aspects.extraDescription: Test: POST /api/courses with valid data. Verify the response course object has totalChapters: 0, totalCredits: 0, and chapters: [] (empty array).

aspects.description: PUT /api/courses/:id updates course metadata
aspects.extraDescription: Test: PUT /api/courses/{id} with {"title": "Updated Title"}. Verify 200 OK, the response course object contains the updated title, and the change persists (GET /api/courses reflects the new title). Note :id is the numeric primary key, not the slug.

aspects.description: Partial updates are supported in PUT (only supplied fields are updated)
aspects.extraDescription: Test: PUT /api/courses/{id} with only {"slug": "new-valid-slug"}. Verify only the slug changes while title, description, and difficulty remain unchanged. Verify an empty body returns 400.

aspects.description: totalChapters and totalCredits are recomputed from chapter data after PUT
aspects.extraDescription: Verify: After a successful PUT, the response and subsequent GET expose totalChapters and totalCredits recomputed from the chapters table (consistent with existing chapter rows), not stale pre-update values.

aspects.description: PUT /api/courses/:id returns 200 with updated course and chapters summary
aspects.extraDescription: Test: PUT /api/courses/{id} with valid data. Verify 200 OK and a response course object with a chapters array (summaries in orderIndex order, same shape as GET /api/courses).

aspects.description: 400 returned for invalid payload or malformed id in course administration
aspects.extraDescription: Test: POST /api/courses missing title or slug returns 400 with code INVALID_COURSE_PAYLOAD. PUT /api/courses/{id} with an invalid difficulty returns 400 with INVALID_DIFFICULTY. PUT with a non-numeric id returns 400 with INVALID_ID_FORMAT.

aspects.description: 404 returned when course ID does not exist in PUT /api/courses/:id
aspects.extraDescription: Test: PUT /api/courses/99999 (a non-existent numeric id) with a valid Bearer token and valid body. Verify 404 with code COURSE_NOT_FOUND.

aspects.description: All responses include Content-Type: application/json header
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Check the Content-Type response header on at least three endpoints (GET /health, GET /api/courses, and one error response). All must be application/json.

aspects.description: Bearer token is correctly parsed from the Authorization header
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Send Authorization: Bearer <valid-token> and verify success. Send the token without the Bearer prefix and verify 401. Send the token in a non-standard header (e.g. X-Token) and verify 401.

aspects.description: Correct HTTP success status codes used (200 for GET/PUT, 201 for POST course creation)
aspects.extraDescription: Test: Verify GET /api/courses returns 200, GET /api/courses/:slug returns 200, GET /api/courses/:slug/chapters/:id returns 200, POST /api/courses returns 201, PUT /api/courses/:id returns 200, and POST .../quiz/validate returns 200.

aspects.description: Overall API response quality and adherence to the OpenAPI specification
judgementScoreDescription: Responses deviate significantly from the OpenAPI spec: missing required fields, incorrect data types, wrong nesting structure, or extra fields that should not appear (e.g. isCorrect exposed)
Responses partially match the OpenAPI spec with minor structural issues: some fields missing or incorrectly named, but core structure is recognizable
Responses closely follow the OpenAPI spec with only minor deviations: all major fields present with correct types, structure is correct, naming is mostly consistent
Responses fully conform to the OpenAPI specification: all fields present with correct types and naming, correct wrapping (e.g. course vs courses key), consistent camelCase, no extraneous fields
aspects.extraDescription: Holistic judgement made by spot-checking responses across all endpoints against assets/api/ssa-content-service-openapi.yaml, complementing the individual measurement aspects above.

aspects.description: GET /api/courses is accessible without authentication
aspects.extraDescription: Test: GET /api/courses with no Authorization header. Verify 200 OK (not 401). This endpoint must be public per specification.

aspects.description: All content endpoints (except health and GET /api/courses) enforce Bearer token authentication
aspects.extraDescription: Test the following endpoints without an Authorization header; each must return 401: GET /api/courses/:slug, GET /api/courses/:slug/chapters/:id, POST /api/courses/:slug/chapters/:id/quiz/validate, POST /api/courses, PUT /api/courses/:id.
